seoTitle: Maudsland vs Maxwelton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Maudsland and Maxwelton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Maudsland and Maxwelton.
heading: Maudsland vs Maxwelton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Maudsland, Queensland and Maxwelton, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Maxwelton has a lower overall climate risk score (26/100) compared to Maudsland (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Maudsland or Maxwelton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Maudsland has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Maxwelton scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Maudsland scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Maxwelton scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Maudsland or Maxwelton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Maxwelton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
